Dolphin Watching Routes

Embark on a journey to discover the most charismatic inhabitants of the Alboran Sea.

  1. Home
  2. Boating Blog
  3. Dolphin Routes

Routes Nature

Dolphin & Whale Watching in Costa del Sol: Best Port Routes

Rumbomed Team

Marine Experience Experts
March 4, 2026 5 min read

Table of Contents

  • Introduction
  • Benalmádena Route
  • Marbella & Banús
  • Estepona & The Strait

The Costa del Sol is more than just luxury and beaches; it is a vital corridor for marine life. At Rumbomed, we take you to the heart of the Alboran Sea to witness dolphins and whales in their natural habitat.

From the playful Common Dolphin to the majestic Fin Whale, our routes are designed to maximize spotting opportunities while respecting the marine environment.

"There is nothing quite like the sound of a dolphin's blowhole breaking the silence of the Mediterranean morning."

Rumbomed Expedition Leader

Benalmádena: The Family Favorite

Departing from the award-winning Puerto Marina, this route is known for its high concentration of Common and Striped dolphins. The coastal shelf here provides the perfect feeding ground.

Strategic Spotting

High success rate within just 2 miles of the coast.

Family Friendly

Calmer waters ideal for children and first-time sailors.

Marbella & Banús: Deep Sea Wonders

Sailing from Marbella allows us to reach greater depths quickly. Here, we often encounter the Bottlenose Dolphin, known for its size and curiosity towards boats.

During the summer months, keep your eyes on the horizon; the deep trenches in this area are part of the migratory path for larger cetaceans.

Estepona: The Gateway to the Strait

Estepona offers our most adventurous route. Its proximity to the Strait of Gibraltar means nutrient-rich currents that attract the "giants" of the sea.

Species Variety

  • Rorqual Whales (seasonal)
  • Large pods of Common Dolphins
  • Flying Fish and Sunfish

Scenery

  • Views of the African Coast
  • The Rock of Gibraltar backdrop
  • Crystal clear "deep blue" waters
